h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to systematically apply the food-to-eater arrow rule to the remaining arrows on the worksheet.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor maintains the same instructional trajectory and does not undergo a dramatic strategy reversal.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor maintains the same instructional trajectory and does not undergo a dramatic strategy reversal.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor maintains the same instructional trajectory and does not undergo a dramatic strategy reversal.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: So both blanks can say 2, but the first 2 is one edge, like 3 to 6, and the second 2 is the whole border, like 12 to 24.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will resolve their confusion about why two different scales have the same numerical value.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep the complaint. The bare 2 is not enough. With the labels covered, say which 2 compares 3 to 6 and which 2 compares 12 to 24.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor uses physical object work (covering labels with a pencil) to validate and deepen the learner's complaint about identical numbers.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Keep the complaint. The bare 2 is not enough. With the labels covered, say which 2 compares 3 to 6 and which 2 compares 12 to 24.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor uses physical object work (covering labels with a pencil) to validate and deepen the learner's complaint about identical numbers.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Keep the complaint. The bare 2 is not enough. With the labels covered, say which 2 compares 3 to 6 and which 2 compares 12 to 24.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor uses physical object work (covering labels with a pencil) to validate and deepen the learner's complaint about identical numbers.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Wait, the grid is showing four of the old square inside it, not two.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would persist in calling the doubled area 'double' without resolving the grid contradiction.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Hold both facts. The side is double. The area is not.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor validates the learner's observation and introduces a structured counting method to resolve the conflict.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot turn occurs, leaving no revised framing for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot turn occurs, leaving no revised framing for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Hold both facts. The side is double. The area is not.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor validates the learner's observation and introduces a structured counting method to resolve the conflict.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Hold both facts. The side is double. The area is not.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor validates the learner's observation and introduces a structured counting method to resolve the conflict.
